title: CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION - 211info
text: OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Enforces laws to ensure everyone has equal access at work, protects against discrimination, and shields workers who report safety problems or use leave. They also protect people who are looking for housing or using public places like stores and transportation. 1800 SW 1st Avenue Suite 500 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 245-3844 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (971) 245-3844 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information or to report discrimination on the basis of a protected class or activity FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: State of Oregon TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: NE 7th Avenue MAX Station (blue, red, and green lines), Portland Streetcar NE Grand and Hoyt, TriMet bus lines 8 and 70 (multiple stops) Services: Constitutional/Civil Rights Groups Education Discrimination Assistance Employment Discrimination Assistance Housing Discrimination Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - WAGE AND HOUR DIVISION EMPLOYMENT - APPRENTICESHIP AND TRAINING DIVISION LEGAL SERVICES - TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE FOR EMPLOYERS PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - CHILD CARE FOR CONSTRUCTION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES
